target.name: Mammoth Biosciences
target.founder: Jennifer Doudna, Trevor Martin, Janice Chen, Lucas Harrington

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $600M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $810M - $5.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.7x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Mammoth Biosciences, a Series D biotech company founded by Jennifer Doudna and co-founders, has raised $600M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of eight private-stage companies in Biotech & Health alongside three public comparables (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L), though the public comp method cannot be applied due to lack of disclosed revenue.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.5x step-up multiplier to the $600M total raised proxy, implying a $900M valuation. Peer-median analysis of five same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies (Abridge at $5.3B, Ultima Genomics at $4.0B, Insitro at $2.4B, Colossal Biosciences at $1.5B, and New Limit at $810M) yields a median valuation of $2.4B, with a range of $810M to $5.3B. The median public biotech P/S multiple of 6.7x across sector comparables cannot anchor a valuation without Mammoth's revenue data.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $765M (low) to $2.8B (high), with a median of $1.6B. The wide dispersion—driven primarily by heterogeneity in peer-stage maturity and capital efficiency—reflects uncertainty around the company's commercialization trajectory and competitive positioning within the crowded frontier biotech landscape. Revenue visibility and clarity on clinical/regulatory milestones would materially narrow this range.
